jokes.text: Except his shoes don't fit. But, I did finally got into Dad's pants. Although I had to have the crotch taken out a little bit.
jokes.setup: Gob wearing his father's suits to look presidential
jokes.punchline: Double entendre about literally getting into Dad's pants with crotch alteration
jokes.explanation: Brilliant layered wordplay with physical comedy implications. Perfect character-specific delivery from Gob's narcissistic obliviousness.

jokes.text: Worse that can happen is I can spill some on my $3,000 suit. Come on!
jokes.setup: Gob's obsession with expensive suits
jokes.punchline: First instance of escalating suit price running gag
jokes.explanation: Sets up the brilliant escalating suit price gag that becomes iconic. Perfect character integration showing Gob's vanity.

jokes.text: They used to roast Dad 'cause they thought that he had a sense of humor about himself, but he didn't, and neither do you.
jokes.setup: Michael correcting Gob about office party dynamics
jokes.punchline: Revealing both father and son's complete lack of self-awareness
jokes.explanation: Good character work exposing family dysfunction. The irony of Michael's own lack of humor is subtle but effective.

jokes.text: Into the kitty! He is awesome. Meow.
jokes.setup: Employee commenting on George being 'into the kitty' (embezzling)
jokes.punchline: Taking it literally with cat sound effect
jokes.explanation: Simple pun that works but lacks sophistication. Gets a chuckle but not memorable.

jokes.text: I've never thought of you that way. / That's sweet.
jokes.setup: Lindsay worried about seeming like a mother
jokes.punchline: George Michael's innocent response revealing family dysfunction
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment showing dysfunctional family dynamics. George Michael's innocence makes it both sweet and disturbing.

jokes.text: I will be head to toe in blue makeup until 8:01 in case the Blue Man Group needs their understudy
jokes.setup: Tobias explaining why he can't attend party
jokes.punchline: His delusional commitment to being Blue Man Group understudy
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Tobias delusion. The specificity of 8:01 and 'understudy' makes it perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: People hear the name Tobias, they think-- big black guy. Well, obviously, I'm not a big guy.
jokes.setup: Tobias explaining his name's intimidation factor
jokes.punchline: Completely missing the racial implication, focusing on size
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character comedy showing Tobias's obliviousness. The misdirection from race to size is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: It's Ann. / It's good. Have fun. Boy's pulling away from you. Are we having yams?
jokes.setup: George Michael correcting pronunciation of girlfriend's name
jokes.punchline: Michael's passive-aggressive response and Gob's non-sequitur about yams
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Michael's hurt and Gob's typical disconnection. Sets up Ann running gag.

jokes.text: You complained all the time, and she was my only wife. And she died.
jokes.setup: Gob comparing situations with in-laws
jokes.punchline: Michael's matter-of-fact correction about his deceased wife
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of dark information. The economy of 'And she died' is brutally effective.

jokes.text: That means she's not getting any from my brother Oscar.
jokes.setup: Lucille sounding tense on the phone
jokes.punchline: Gob's inappropriate sexual analysis of his mother
jokes.explanation: Typical Gob inappropriateness but not particularly clever. Gets cringe laughs from family dysfunction.

jokes.text: First I blow him, then I poke him. Guy has no idea what he's in for.
jokes.setup: Lucille describing her defense against intruders
jokes.punchline: Unintentional double entendre about blowing and poking
jokes.explanation: Brilliant unintentional sexual innuendo from prim Lucille. The innocence makes it funnier than intentional raunch.

jokes.text: He calls it a quest to recharge his chi. I call it an excuse for him to score his pot and wag his pickle at coeds.
jokes.setup: Narrator explaining Oscar's annual walkabout
jokes.punchline: Crude but accurate description of Oscar's real motivations
jokes.explanation: 'Wag his pickle' is memorably crude phrasing. Narrator's dry delivery of vulgar content works well.

jokes.text: Oh, that's ridiculous. I want you to move in with me.
jokes.setup: Michael suggesting she's acting out for attention
jokes.punchline: Immediately proving his point with demanding behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic structure. Lucille immediately validates Michael's theory with zero self-awareness.

jokes.text: Michael, a mother doesn't have a child to give herself company. Annyong was to teach Buster a lesson.
jokes.setup: Michael pointing out she adopted for company
jokes.punchline: Lucille's self-righteous correction revealing equally selfish motive
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille logic - correcting Michael while revealing an equally selfish motivation. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: I can't change a hundred.
jokes.setup: Buster taking his first bus ride
jokes.punchline: Sheltered Buster only having large bills
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective character moment showing Buster's sheltered upbringing. Visual tells the story efficiently.

jokes.text: That could be good for Army.
jokes.setup: Buster seeing skill crane game
jokes.punchline: His naive assumption it has military applications
jokes.explanation: Perfect Buster innocence. The idea that arcade skills translate to military training is endearingly naive.

jokes.text: That's about the worst thing I've ever heard. / I had to drive because she had a little bit of a buzz on.
jokes.setup: Maeby explaining being signed out early
jokes.punchline: Revealing the mother was drunk, making situation worse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure. Each revelation makes Lindsay's parenting worse, culminating in drunk driving implications.

jokes.text: I just don't want people's kids getting their sticky little fingers all over these 26,000 dollar pants.
jokes.setup: Suit price gag continues
jokes.punchline: Price quadruples to $26,000 specifically for pants
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with specific focus on pants. The astronomical price increase is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: So, should-should... should, uh, should... Should-should... Sh-sh-sh... Should-Should... should, uh, should...
jokes.setup: Gob trying to form a coherent response
jokes.punchline: Complete verbal breakdown showing his inability to handle conflict
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Gob's inability to handle pushback. The stammering is perfectly performed cringe.

jokes.text: Please refrain from discussing or engaging in any sort of interoffice (bleep)ing or (bleep)ing of finger (bleep) or (bleep)sting or (bleep)ing or even (bleep). Even though so many so many people in this office are begging for it.
jokes.setup: Gob giving sexual harassment speech
jokes.punchline: Completely inappropriate speech that creates more harassment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob move - trying to prevent harassment while being completely inappropriate. The bleeps make it funnier.

jokes.text: I'll take off my pants, I'll shave my (bleep). And I'll personally (bleep)...
jokes.setup: Gob threatening anyone who approaches Lindsay
jokes.punchline: Escalating to bizarre and inappropriate threats
jokes.explanation: Typical Gob escalation into absurdity. The threats make no logical sense which makes them funnier.

jokes.text: Everybody dance now!
jokes.setup: Gob trying to start the party
jokes.punchline: Awkward silence and lack of response from employees
jokes.explanation: Standard cringe moment. The silence after his enthusiastic command is predictably awkward but effective.

jokes.text: There's no shame in being a coward. / A coward? I'm not a coward.
jokes.setup: Buster returning home, Lucille comforting him
jokes.punchline: Her 'comfort' is calling him a coward
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille mothering - insulting while trying to comfort. Buster's predictable defensive response is character-perfect.

jokes.text: The seal is for marksmanship, and the gorilla is for sand racing.
jokes.setup: Buster showing off his 'Army awards'
jokes.punchline: They're clearly carnival/arcade prizes, not military honors
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual gag with perfect character logic. Buster's earnest presentation of carnival prizes as military honors is peak comedy.

jokes.text: Now if you'll excuse me, they're putting me in something called Hero Squad.
jokes.setup: Buster claiming military advancement
jokes.punchline: Obvious reference to arcade game, not real military unit
jokes.explanation: Perfect extension of the arcade gag. 'Hero Squad' sounds like a video game, maintaining Buster's delusion perfectly.

jokes.text: How the worm has turned.
jokes.setup: Discussing ungrateful children
jokes.punchline: Lucille's malapropism of 'how the tables have turned'
jokes.explanation: Classic Lucille malapropism. The confidence with which she delivers the wrong phrase is character-perfect.

jokes.text: You have 101 unheard messages. First message.
jokes.setup: Michael calling Tobias
jokes.punchline: Absurd number of unheard messages
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the rapid-fire message sequence. 101 messages immediately signals absurd neglect of technology.

jokes.text: Oh, if you're worried about my husband, it's fine. He's no bigger than a Cub Scout. You could take him, and you could certainly take me.
jokes.setup: Lindsay flirting with employee despite harassment warning
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate invitation while insulting her husband
jokes.explanation: Good Lindsay character work - completely missing social cues while being inappropriately forward. Tobias insult is character-consistent.

jokes.text: This has been pleasant and professional. Good luck in the coming business year.
jokes.setup: Lindsay's inappropriate flirting
jokes.punchline: Employee's stiff, formal rejection after harassment warning
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response to Lindsay's advances. The overly formal language shows fear of Gob's threats.

jokes.text: It is the worst Christmas party I've ever been to in my life. Now, watch this.
jokes.setup: Lindsay complaining about the party
jokes.punchline: She's about to make it worse with karaoke
jokes.explanation: Sets up the karaoke disaster. Lindsay's confidence before making things worse is typical character behavior.

jokes.text: Jesus, bleibet meine Freude / Meines Herzens Trost / Jesus wehret / Allem Leide...
jokes.setup: George Michael at Ann's family Christmas party
jokes.punchline: Creepy German religious singing that George Michael finds disturbing
jokes.explanation: Good cultural cringe humor. The formal German hymn creates effective contrast with normal Christmas party expectations.

jokes.text: No, no, they said I could leave for the second hour of silent prayer.
jokes.setup: George Michael explaining why he could leave Ann's party
jokes.punchline: Revealing how extremely religious and boring the party is
jokes.explanation: Perfect detail that explains the religious family's extreme nature. 'Second hour' implies multiple hours of silence.

jokes.text: that 'Afternoon Delight' was more adult-themed than its innocent melody would have you believe.
jokes.setup: Michael and Maeby singing innocent-seeming song
jokes.punchline: Narrator revealing the song's sexual content
jokes.explanation: Brilliant setup for the main cringe sequence. The narrator's dry delivery makes the inappropriate duet funnier.

jokes.text: And the thought of rubbing you is getting so exciting / Skyrockets in flight...
jokes.setup: Uncle and niece unknowingly singing sexual lyrics
jokes.punchline: Increasingly inappropriate lyrics between family members
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe comedy. The innocence of the performers makes the sexual content devastatingly uncomfortable and hilarious.

jokes.text: Seven grand-- you better believe I love them. I mean, look... look what you're wearing. You look like crap.
jokes.setup: Employee complimenting Gob's suits
jokes.punchline: Gob inflating price and immediately insulting the complimenter
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob response - accepting compliment then immediately being cruel. Suit price continues to escalate absurdly.

jokes.text: (bleep) you, Tom.
jokes.setup: Tom trying to give genuine compliment about magic
jokes.punchline: Gob's completely inappropriate response to kindness
jokes.explanation: Classic Gob overreaction. Responding to genuine compliment with hostility is perfectly character-consistent.

jokes.text: No, you know what, you're fired. No, you're all fired. Each and every one of you. Merry Christmas.
jokes.setup: Gob's growing frustration with employees
jokes.punchline: Mass firing at Christmas party with holiday greeting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob nuclear option. The juxtaposition of mass firing with 'Merry Christmas' is darkly hilarious.

jokes.text: The guy who's dirty dancing with his niece is going to tell the guy in the 36...hundred dollar suit how to run the business. Come on.
jokes.setup: Gob defending his firing decision
jokes.punchline: Suit price reaches $3,600 while accusing Michael of inappropriate behavior
jokes.explanation: Great combination of running gags. The 'dirty dancing' callback adds another layer to the cringe while suit prices continue.

jokes.text: I walk in and there's a colored man in my kitchen. / "Colored"? What color was he exactly? / Blue.
jokes.setup: Lucille describing break-in with outdated racial term
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation reveals it was blue-painted Tobias
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant misdirection. Takes Lucille's problematic language and subverts it perfectly with literal blue man callback.

jokes.text: Um... maybe a little "Afternoon Delight"?
jokes.setup: Michael asking what his mother needs
jokes.punchline: Maeby innocently suggesting the sexual song they just performed
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that adds another layer of inappropriateness. Maeby's innocence makes the suggestion funnier and more cringe.

jokes.text: Oscar thought that Michael was referring to a particular brand of cannabis named "Afternoon Deelite," a strain famous for slowing behavior.
jokes.setup: Michael and Maeby's innocent suggestion
jokes.punchline: Oscar interpreting it as marijuana reference
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that creates new comedy from the song title. The marijuana interpretation adds perfect Oscar character consistency.

jokes.text: which way do I try to get it in her? / I don't need any details. / Maybe I'll put it in her brownie.
jokes.setup: Oscar discussing marijuana delivery methods
jokes.punchline: Unintentional sexual innuendo about his mother
jokes.explanation: Good double entendre humor with family dysfunction undertones. Michael's horror at the innuendo adds another layer.

jokes.text: Oh, I got blown! So I can't sleep!
jokes.setup: Tobias explaining his head injury
jokes.punchline: Unintentional sexual innuendo about being 'blown'
jokes.explanation: Classic Tobias unintentional innuendo. His complete innocence while using sexual language is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Sure-sure, the guy in the $600 banana suit. Come on!
jokes.setup: Gob in banana costume
jokes.punchline: Suit price gag continues even in ridiculous costume
jokes.explanation: Brilliant extension of running gag. Applying the expensive suit logic to a banana costume is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: And I think I might try nibbling a little afternoon delight... / That's my wife and nephew!
jokes.setup: Lindsay singing same sexual song
jokes.punchline: Tobias's horror at seeing his family singing sexual lyrics
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that multiplies the cringe. Having another family member unknowingly sing sexual lyrics makes it exponentially worse.

jokes.text: We have an open relationship!
jokes.setup: Tobias seeing what he thinks is his wife cheating
jokes.punchline: His desperate excuse shouted to strangers
jokes.explanation: Good Tobias character moment showing his desperation to save face even in crisis. The public declaration is perfectly awkward.

jokes.text: Hey, Mikey, relax. It's just a giant banana.
jokes.setup: Car accident with person in banana suit
jokes.punchline: Oscar's casual dismissal due to costume
jokes.explanation: Perfect Oscar casualness about danger. The logic that costume makes injury less serious is absurdly character-appropriate.

jokes.text: They're laughing with me, Michael. They're laughing with me.
jokes.setup: Gob stuck in banana suit getting rescued
jokes.punchline: His delusion that humiliation equals approval
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to earlier theme. Gob's desperate need to believe he's respected makes his delusion both sad and funny.

jokes.text: Hey, it's Tobias. Who wants to take me to the hospital?
jokes.setup: Tobias has been injured in accident
jokes.punchline: His casual, polite request for medical help
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias politeness in crisis. Treating serious injury as mild inconvenience is character-consistent understatement.

jokes.text: Does not seem like it would be that dirty. / It is catchy. That's the thing.
jokes.setup: Father and son discussing the song
jokes.punchline: Both still missing the sexual content while admitting it's catchy
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing their continued innocence. The admission that it's catchy despite being inappropriate is perfectly naive.

jokes.text: Unfortunately, he can't hear it and his life stays the same.
jokes.setup: Blue Man Group finally calling Tobias
jokes.punchline: His hearing damage prevents him from receiving life-changing opportunity
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic ending. The convergence of multiple plot threads into cruel irony is expertly constructed dark comedy.
